mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer confidence and demand, driving positive market returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations yield negative forward returns in greater than 50 percent of cases across any 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that absorbs rate hikes, generating short-term positive equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day returns in greater than 60 percent of tightening periods over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations, producing positive forward equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over any 5-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above threshold consistently precede positive 20-day returns in greater than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 55 percent of observations over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during a late-cycle expansion signals peak consumer spending and impending demand contraction, causing short-term negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines in late-cycle periods are followed by positive 5-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.
